repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dist_synth_control_v1 downgraded: convex-hull sufficient condition and marginal counterfactual quantile/CDF identification

published_prior_art.cite: Gunsilius2023DiSCo and Chen2020DistributionalSC identify marginal counterfactual distributions/quantile curves
Callaway2021BoundsDTEPanel plus FanPark2010 sharp bounds: DTE depends on unknown joint distribution and admits Frechet-type sharp bounds
AtheyImbens2006CIC and CallawayLi2019QTEDID identify distributional/QTT objects under rank or copula/dependence restrictions
AtheyImbens2006CIC and CallawayLi2019QTEDID: rank/coplanar dependence restrictions deliver plug-in quantile-effect identification

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: Kline2020BoundsDTEPanel is not Patrick Kline 2020; the JoE article is Brantly Callaway 2021, and Masini2025DCA appears as JoE 249(PA), not volume 246.

novelty_flags.label: Kline2020BoundsDTEPanel
novelty_flags.one_line: The body repeatedly compares to 'Kline', but the verified JoE paper with this title is Brantly Callaway 2021; the named comparator is misidentified.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest missing comparator is Fan and Park 2010, 'Sharp Bounds on the Distribution of Treatment Effects and Their Statistical Inference', which directly treats sharp bounds for treatment-effect distributions from marginals.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the flagship claim cites Callaway/Kline, Gunsilius, and Chen without a section/equation/page/open-problem anchor showing the specific DiSCo copula gap is unpublished.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The rank/copula taxonomy cites Athey-Imbens and Callaway-Li by bibkey only; a flagship frontier needs location-level anchors to the exact rank/dependence assumptions being separated.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption no-copula already asserts that every coupling is admissible while §10 treats that pasting step as the open kernel; move the admissibility claim into the conjecture or weaken the assumption.

soundness_flags.label: Degenerate-effect reduction
soundness_flags.one_line: The corollary must add the compatibility condition F1(y)=F0(y-tau); otherwise the constant-effect restriction can be inconsistent with the stated marginals, and the unrestricted Theta_I(P) does not literally collapse.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Kline2020BoundsDTEPanel with a corrected Callaway2021 bibkey throughout, and add Fan-Park/Firpo-Ridder style sharp-bound comparators to show exactly what is not already classical Frechet-Makarov partial identification.

Recast Conjecture 1 as a strict iff frontier against a location-anchored DiSCo statement: marginal DSC identifies F0 iff the estimand is marginal, while F_Delta is point identified iff a named copula condition is imposed.

Add a computable finite-support LP/OT diagnostic with explicit dimension and constraints, plus an empirical reporting object such as bound width for Pr{Delta <= d}; this would make the field-tier kernel closer to flagship methodology.
Patch the degenerate-effect corollary by conditioning on compatible shifted marginals and separating the restricted identified set from the unrestricted coupling set.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal around a corrected Callaway/Fan-Park comparator chain, fix the smuggled no-copula assumption, and add location-level anchors before rerunning Stage -0.5.
